scenario: You're enrolled in Professor Lastman's European History course, which meets on Tuesdays and Thursdays. You were absent last Thursday because of a scheduled medical appointment, and as a result you missed a short in-class quiz that counts toward your participation grade.
direction: Write an email to Professor Lastman. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you were absent and that you understand you missed the quiz.
Ask whether you can make up the quiz or complete an alternate assignment.
Ask if there's anything else you should review to stay caught up.
subject: Missed quiz on Thursday

scenario: You rent an apartment from Greenview Properties. Since yesterday evening, there has been a steady leak from the ceiling in your living room near the window. You have already put a bucket under the leak, but the water is starting to damage the wall and you are worried it could affect the electrical outlet nearby.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Park. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the problem clearly, including when it started and where the leak is.
Ask for a maintenance visit as soon as possible and ask to be told a specific time when they will arrive.
Ask what you should do to prevent further damage until maintenance arrives.
subject: Ceiling leak in living room

scenario: You rent an apartment from Lakeside Residences. For the past three weeks, your upstairs neighbors have been hosting loud gatherings late at night, often continuing past midnight. The noise from music, loud conversations, and footsteps makes it very difficult for you to sleep, especially on weekdays when you have to wake up early for work.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Delgado. In your email, do the following:
goals: Clearly explain the situation, including how long it has been happening, what kind of noise it is, and how it affects your daily life.
Describe what you have already done to try to solve the problem on your own.
Ask what actions the property management can take to address the issue, and what you should do if the problem continues.
subject: Late-night noise from upstairs

scenario: You recently ordered a pair of wireless headphones from an online electronics store called TechWorld. The package arrived yesterday, but when you opened it, you found a different, cheaper model than the one you selected on the website. You have not used the headphones yet and still have all the original packaging.
direction: Write an email to TechWorld's customer service department. In your email, do the following:
goals: Clearly explain the problem, including what you ordered, what you received, and when the package arrived.
Ask how they will resolve the issue, and whether there will be any additional cost to you.
Request a specific timeline for when you can expect a reply and a solution.
subject: Wrong item received

scenario: You are a student in Professor Kim's sociology class. Last week, you submitted your midterm essay via the class website. Yesterday, you checked your grade and were surprised to see it was much lower than you expected. When you opened the file that was graded, you realized it was an older draft of your essay, not the final version. You still have the correct final version saved on your computer.
direction: Write an email to Professor Kim. In your email, do the following:
goals: Clearly explain the problem and how you noticed that the wrong version was graded.
Ask if you can send the final version and have your grade changed.
Ask when you can expect a reply.
subject: Wrong essay version graded

scenario: You are working on a group project in your political science class. Unfortunately, you will not be able to attend the next group meeting because you have an appointment with your dentist at the same time. You are unable to reschedule the appointment because the dentist doesn't have any time available this month.
direction: Write an email to John Shepherd. In your email, do the following:
goals: Clearly explain the problem, including the fact that you cannot change date of the appointment
Ask John to explain the situation to the other members of the group when the meeting takes place
Request that John, or another group member, send you a summary of what is discussed at the meeting
subject: Cannot attend next group meeting

scenario: Two weeks ago, you booked a room at the Gateway Hotel in Florida for a vacation you will take in the spring. Since then, your best friend Sarah has decided to join you on the trip. She has already purchased an airplane ticket, but has not yet reserved her own hotel room. She would like to stay at the same hotel as you.
direction: Write an email to Gary Bembridge. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that your friend will join your vacation, and that she would like to stay in the hotel as well
Request that the room be on the same floor as yours, if possible.
Thank Gary for taking care of this as soon as possible since you will be arriving in just a few days.
subject: Adding a room to my reservation

scenario: You ordered a jacket from an online store, but when it arrived, you found that they sent the wrong size. You need the jacket for a family event next week, so you want to contact customer service as soon as possible.
direction: Write an email to the customer service department. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the problem with your order
Ask whether the correct size can be sent quickly
Request information about how to return the wrong item
subject: Wrong jacket size received

scenario: You are planning a short holiday next month and found an advertisement for a hotel that looks suitable. However, the advertisement does not clearly say whether breakfast is included or if there is parking available. You want to get this information before booking.
direction: Write an email to the hotel owner. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you are interested in the hotel
Ask whether breakfast and parking are included
Request information about the easiest way to make a booking
subject: Question before booking

scenario: You are a university student and usually work on assignments in the campus library in the evenings. The library is a useful place to study because it is quiet, and contains useful books and journals. Recently, the library has been closing earlier than usual, which has made it difficult for you to complete your assignments.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Severin. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why the library is important for your studies
Ask why the opening hours have changed
Request information about whether the library will stay open later again
subject: Library closing hours

scenario: You are a student at a university, and you would like to take part in a volunteer group that helps new international students adjust to campus life. You saw a notice about the group on a campus board, but it did not include enough details about the activities or how students can become members.
direction: Write an email to Richard Moss. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you are interested in joining the volunteer group
Ask about the activities the group organizes
Request information about how to become a member
subject: Interested in joining the volunteer group

scenario: You recently ordered a small bookshelf online, but when it arrived, one of the shelves was cracked and several screws were missing. You checked the company's website, but the instructions for requesting a replacement were unclear.
direction: Write an email to Olivia Grant. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the item you received
Explain what you've done so far to solve the problem
Ask what steps you should take to receive a replacement
subject: Damaged bookshelf - replacement request

scenario: You recently joined a local gym, but after your first week, you noticed that some of the exercise machines are often unavailable in the evening. You also heard that the gym may offer special classes and quieter hours, but you could not find clear information online.
direction: Write an email to Karen Patel. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention which times you usually visit the gym
Suggest one way the gym could improve the equipment situation
Find out whether the gym offers any beginner fitness classes
subject: Equipment availability and classes

scenario: You attended a guest lecture on campus by Dr. Amina Rashid on Monday about how to stay productive and manage stress as a college student. You wanted to ask a follow-up question after the talk, but there were too many students waiting to speak with her.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Amina Rashid. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that you attended her lecture and briefly mention what you found especially helpful.
Ask if she could share more information about the techniques she talked about.
Ask if she has any suggestions for resources you could use to improve your study habits this semester.
subject: Follow-up question from Monday's lecture

scenario: You recently attended a workshop on public speaking organized by your university's career center. The workshop was helpful, but you noticed that some topics were not covered. You would like to share your experience with the workshop coordinator, Ms. Nakamura.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Nakamura. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe what you found most useful about the workshop
Explain what topics you think were missing
Suggest an idea for a future workshop
subject: Feedback on the public speaking workshop

scenario: Your Chemistry 201 professor announced that the midterm has been moved up by one week. You have a conflict with the new date.
direction: Write an email to Professor Martinez. In your email, do the following:
goals: Acknowledge the change to the midterm date
Explain your scheduling conflict
Propose a solution
subject: Conflict with new midterm date

scenario: Your new roommate plays loud music late at night, which is making it hard for you to sleep and study.
direction: Write an email to Jordan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem
Explain the impact it is having on you
Suggest a compromise
subject: Late-night music

scenario: The library sent a survey asking readers for feedback on its new study room booking system.
direction: Write an email to the Library Services Team. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention one thing you like about the new booking system
Describe one problem you had with it
Make one suggestion for improvement
subject: Feedback on the room booking system

scenario: You want to join Professor Kim's undergraduate research group.
direction: Write an email to Professor Kim. In your email, do the following:
goals: Introduce yourself
State your interest in the research group
Request a meeting
subject: Interest in your research group

scenario: You are a student at a university. The campus library is hosting a study skills workshop next week. You attended the same workshop last semester and found it very helpful, and you want to recommend that your friend attend.
direction: Write an email to Alex. In your email, do the following:
goals: Recommend the workshop
Explain the specific reasons you found it helpful
Encourage your friend to sign up
subject: You should go to this workshop
